94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
95	
96	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
97	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
98	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
99	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
100	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
101	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
102	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
103	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
104	
105	namespace PresheafOfModules
106	
107	universe v'
108	
109	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
113	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
114	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
115	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
116	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
117	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
118	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
119	  naturality {X Y} f := by
120	    ext r
121	    dsimp
122	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
123	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
124	    simp only [map_one]
125	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
129	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
130	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
131	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
132	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
133	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
134	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
135	  app X := by
136	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
137	  naturality {X Y} f := by
138	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
139	    dsimp
140	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
141	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
142	    rfl
143	
144	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
145	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
146	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
147	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
148	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
149	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
150	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
151	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
152	  μ_natural_left := by
153	    intro X Y f X'
154	    ext1 Z
155	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
156	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
157	  μ_natural_right := by
158	    intro X Y X' f
159	    ext1 Z
160	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
161	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
162	  associativity := by
163	    intro M N P
164	    ext1 Z
165	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
166	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
167	  left_unitality := by
168	    intro M
169	    ext1 Z
170	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
171	      (M.obj Z)
172	  right_unitality := by
173	    intro M
174	    ext1 Z
175	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
176	      (M.obj Z)
177	
178	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
179	sheafification localizer
180	
181	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
182	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
183	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
184	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
185	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
186	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
187	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
188	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
189	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
190	structure. -/
191	
192	section FlatWhisker
193	
194	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
195	
196	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
197	
198	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
199	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
200	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
201	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
202	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
203	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
204	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
205	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
206	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
207	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
208	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
209	
210	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
211	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
212	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
213	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
214	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
215	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
216	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
217	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
218	
219	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
220	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
221	half. -/
222	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
223	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
224	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
225	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
226	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
227	  constructor
228	  intro U s
229	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
230	  | zero =>
231	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
232	      intro V i _
233	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
234	  | tmul a b =>
235	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
236	      intro V i hi
237	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
238	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
239	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
240	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
241	      congr 1
242	  | add s t hs ht =>
243	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
244	      intro V i hi
245	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
246	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
247	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
248	
249	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
250	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
251	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
252	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
253	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
254	injectivity half. -/
255	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
256	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
257	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
258	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
259	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
260	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
261	  constructor
262	  intro X ξ η h
263	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
264	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
265	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
266	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
267	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
268	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
269	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
270	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
271	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
272	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
273	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
274	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
275	  have hex : Function.Exact
276	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
277	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
278	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
279	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
280	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
281	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
282	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
283	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
284	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
285	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
286	    intro ζ
287	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
288	    | zero =>
289	        rw [map_zero]
290	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
291	    | tmul a kk =>
292	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
293	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
294	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
295	          rw [map_zero]
296	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
297	          exact kk.2
298	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
299	        intro V f hf
300	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
301	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
302	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
303	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
304	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
305	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
306	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
307	        rw [map_add]
308	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
309	        intro V f hf
310	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
311	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
312	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
313	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
314	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
315	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
316	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
317	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
318	  intro V f hf
319	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
320	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
321	  exact hf
322	
323	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
324	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
325	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
326	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
327	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
328	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
329	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
330	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
331	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
332	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
333	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
334	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
335	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
336	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
337	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
338	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
339	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
340	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
341	
342	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
343	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
344	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
345	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
346	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
347	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
348	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
349	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
350	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
351	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
352	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
353	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
354	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
355	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
356	  have hconj : g ▷ F
357	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
358	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
359	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
360	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
361	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
362	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
363	  exact hwl
364	
365	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
366	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
367	
368	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
369	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
370	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
371	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
372	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
373	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
374	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
375	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
376	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
377	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
378	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
379	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
380	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
381	
382	section WhiskerOfW
383	
384	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
385	
386	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}

388	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
389	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
390	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
391	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
392	
393	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
394	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
395	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
396	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
397	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
398	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
399	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
400	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
401	
402	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
403	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
404	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
405	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
406	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
407	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
408	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
409	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
410	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
411	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
412	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
413	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
414	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
415	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
416	  -- ROUTE (d) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
417	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); needs the module-level stalk/tensor port
418	  -- (d.1)+(d.2), Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. See docstring + task result.
419	  sorry

421	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
422	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
423	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
424	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
425	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
426	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
427	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
428	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
429	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
430	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
431	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
432	  have hbij := hg
433	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
434	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
435	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
436	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
437	
438	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
439	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
440	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
441	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
442	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
443	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
444	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
445	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
446	  have hconj : g ▷ F
447	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
448	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
449	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
450	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
451	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
452	  exact hwl

462	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
463	read at a single morphism. -/
464	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
465	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
466	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
467	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
468	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
469	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
470	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
471	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
472	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
473	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
474	  exact h2

486	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
487	
488	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
489	
490	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
491	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
492	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
493	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
494	
495	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
496	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
497	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
498	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
499	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
500	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
501	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
502	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
503	
504	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
505	
506	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
507	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
508	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
509	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
510	
511	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
512	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
513	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
514	sheafification. -/
515	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
516	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
517	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
518	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
519	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
520	
521	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
522	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
523	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
524	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
525	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
526	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
527	law is definitional. -/
528	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
529	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
530	
531	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
532	
533	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
534	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
535	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
536	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
537	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
538	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
539	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
540	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
541	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
542	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
543	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
544	
545	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
546	
547	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
548	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
549	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
550	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
551	
552	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
553	
554	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
555	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
556	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
557	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
558	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
559	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
560	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
561	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
562	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
563	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
564	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
565	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
566	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
567	
568	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
569	sheaf.**
570	
571	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
572	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
573	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
574	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
575	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
576	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
577	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
578	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
579	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
580	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
581	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
582	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
583	
584	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
585	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
586	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
587	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
588	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
589	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
590	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
591	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
592	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
593	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
594	
595	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
596	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
597	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
598	sheafification counit. -/
599	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
600	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
601	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
602	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
603	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
604	
605	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
606	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
607	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
608	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
609	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
610	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
611	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
612	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
613	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
614	
615	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
616	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
617	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise
618	flat), there is an isomorphism `(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the
619	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law
620	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
621	
622	iter-212 status (typed `sorry`; **go/no-go bridge CLEARED, a NEW residual
623	located**). The construction is the blueprint's three-step composite. Writing
624	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)`,
625	`η = sheafificationAdjunction.unit` (the localizer unit, `toPresheaf`-image
626	`toSheafify ∈ J.W`), and `α` the presheaf-of-modules associator:
627	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso  (P flat ⇒ right-whiskered `η ∈ J.W`
628	     by `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`; `a` inverts `J.W` by `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`),
629	     giving `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P = a(a(M.val⊗N.val).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
630	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`;
631	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso  (M flat), giving
632	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ a(M.val ⊗ a(N.val⊗P.val).val) = M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
633	
634	**The go/no-go bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` is now
635	CLOSED, axiom-clean**, as `PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (it is
636	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` — the
637	sheafification functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` —
638	read at one morphism). The right-whisker conjugate
639	`PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat` is also closed axiom-clean.
640	
641	**The genuine residual is now the flatness feeding steps 1 and 3**: steps 1/3
642	need `J.W (toPresheaf (η ▷ P.val))` / `J.W (toPresheaf (M.val ◁ η))`, which
643	`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` supply ONLY from the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
644	`∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val(U))` (resp. M). This is NOT
645	derivable from `IsInvertible`: the only Mathlib route is single-ring
646	`Module.Invertible R m → Projective → Flat`, which would require `P.val(U)` to be
647	an invertible `𝒪_X(U)`-module for EVERY open `U` — false in general (the global
648	sections functor over a non-affine open is not exact and does not preserve
649	flatness; invertibility is a LOCAL/affine property). The blueprint's
650	"invertible ⇒ projective ⇒ flat" conflates global module-invertibility with
651	sectionwise-over-all-opens flatness. The mathematically-correct fix scopes
652	injectivity LOCALLY (local-triviality whiskering: on the cover where `P ≅ 𝒪`,
653	`η ▷ P ≅ η`, locally injective) — a new lemma needing `IsInvertible ⇒
654	IsLocallyTrivial` (blueprint-flagged nontrivial). A second obstacle is pure Lean
655	friction: `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is only defeq (not syntactically equal) to
656	`X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`, so the unit `η`'s monoidal
657	whiskering and the `Module.Flat` instance both fail typeclass synthesis / hit
658	heartbeat timeouts. See task result for the full corrected decomposition. -/
659	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
660	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N)
661	    (hP : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial P) :
662	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
663	  -- Re-scoped to `IsLocallyTrivial` (decl unprotected). ROUTE (d) three-step
664	  -- composite of the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`; the locally-trivial
665	  -- hypotheses are not even consumed (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
666	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)), but are retained to match the blueprint pin.
667	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
668	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
669	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
670	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
671	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
672	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
673	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
674	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
675	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
676	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
677	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
678	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
679	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
680	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
681	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
682	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
683	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
684	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
685	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
686	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
687	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
688	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
689	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
690	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
691	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
692	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
693	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
694	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
695	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
696	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
697	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
698	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
699	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
700	  have e2 := a.mapIso
701	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
702	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
703	
704	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
705	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
706	
707	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
708	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
709	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
710	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
711	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
712	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
713	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
714	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
715	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
716	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
717	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
718	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
719	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
720	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
721	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
722	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
723	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
724	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
725	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
726	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
727	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
728	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
729	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
730	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
731	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
732	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
733	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
734	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
735	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
736	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
737	
738	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
739

1	# Analogy: does Mathlib supply a monoidal `SheafOfModules` / monoidal sheafification — and the flatness-free, MonoidalClosed-free route the prior 8 iters missed
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ts-monoidal213
8	
9	## Iteration
10	213
11	
12	## Question
13	(1) Does Mathlib provide `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R)` (or `Sheaf J (ModuleCat …)`)?
14	(2) Is `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` a (lax/strong) monoidal functor, or is there
15	a reflective-localization monoidal transport (`Localization.Monoidal`,
16	`MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal`) giving the associator for free? (3) If neither, the minimal
17	correct route to `Nonempty ((M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P))` for invertible objects — does it require
18	EITHER (a) sectionwise flatness `[∀ U, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (M.val(U))]` (FALSE for
19	non-affine opens) OR (b) the abandoned `tensorObj_restrict_iso`? If a route needs NEITHER,
20	name it precisely.
21	
22	## Headline
23	**This REVERSES the prior (iters 206–212) conclusion that the associator is walled behind
24	the absent `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.** The wall was an artifact of how the
25	project characterized the sheafification localizer `J.W`. There is a flatness-free,
26	MonoidalClosed-free, `tensorObj_restrict_iso`-free route — and Mathlib *newly blesses the
27	exact technique* in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (Joël Riou,
28	**2026**), which iters 206–210 did not see (they found only the older MonoidalClosed-gated
29	`GrothendieckTopology.W.monoidal`).
30	
31	All citations below are FRESH LSP/grep-verified against the project's pinned Mathlib
32	(`.lake/packages/mathlib`) this iteration.
33	
34	## Project artifact(s)
35	- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:408` `tensorObj M N := a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val)`, `a = sheafification`.
36	- `:332/:348` `W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` — the (dead) sectionwise-flat whiskering.
37	- `:373` `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` — go/no-go bridge, CLOSED axiom-clean.
38	- `:568` `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (typed `sorry`).
39	- `:633` `tensorObj_restrict_iso` — route-(b) wall (abandoned iter-209).
40	- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex:664–688` — the false "Flatness is free" step.
41	
42	## Decisions identified
43	
44	### Decision 1: does Mathlib supply `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R)`?
45	
46	- **Mathlib idiom (verified).** NO instance for `SheafOfModules`. The general
47	  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` exists (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`,
48	  line 165): `[(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] → MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A)`,
49	  built as the localization-of-monoidal `LocalizedMonoidal (presheafToSheaf J A) J.W`. It is
50	  for `Sheaf J A` with a **fixed** monoidal `A` and the **pointwise** functor-category tensor
51	  `(F ⊗ G).obj X = F.obj X ⊗_A G.obj X`. The project needs the **relative module tensor**
52	  `(M ⊗ᵖ N).obj X = M.obj X ⊗_{R.obj X} N.obj X` (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`), which is a
53	  *different* monoidal structure (a quotient of the ℤ-tensor), so `Sheaf.monoidalCategory`
54	  does not instantiate to `SheafOfModules R`. The file's own header (lines 30–35) already
55	  records this absence.
56	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (no off-the-shelf monoidal `SheafOfModules`).
57	
58	### Decision 2: is `sheafification` monoidal (free associator)?
59	
60	- **Mathlib idiom (verified).** YES, conditionally: `presheafToSheaf J A` carries a
61	  `.Monoidal` instance — `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.instMonoidalFunctorOppositePresheafToSheaf`
62	  (`Sites.Monoidal`), `[(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] → (presheafToSheaf J A).Monoidal` — and
63	  `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.*` (`Localization.Monoidal.Basic`) gives the localized
64	  monoidal category, both **gated on `(J.W).IsMonoidal`** (`[L.IsLocalization W] [W.IsMonoidal]`).
65	  So a *strong-monoidal sheafification* (⇒ free associator) IS available the moment
66	  `J.W.IsMonoidal` holds — again for fixed-A pointwise tensor, not the module relative tensor.
67	- **The pivotal sub-question — how does one get `J.W.IsMonoidal`?** TWO routes in Mathlib:
68	  1. `GrothendieckTopology.W.monoidal` (`Sites.Monoidal:149`) — under
69	     `variable [MonoidalClosed A] [∀ F₁ F₂, HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] …` (lines 125–127);
70	     its `whiskerLeft` (line 132) is literally built from `MonoidalClosed.curry` /
71	     `ihom.adjunction`. **This is the wall iters 206–210 hit** (for modules → the absent
72	     `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`).
73	  2. **`ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W` + the
74	     `HasEnoughPoints` instance** (`Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW`, 2026):
75	     `[J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] [HasEnoughPoints J] → (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal`,
76	     under `A` monoidal-concrete with colimits/products, `forget A` reflecting isos &
77	     preserving filtered colimits, and **`tensorLeft X` / `tensorRight X` preserving filtered
78	     colimits** (true for module categories). **NO `MonoidalClosed`.** The one-line proof is
79	     the whole point: `hP.W_iff` says `J.W f ↔ every fibre (stalk) functor sends f to an iso`,
80	     then `Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor` + `infer_instance` — i.e. **`J.W`-maps are stalkwise
81	     isos, and `iso ⊗ iso` is an iso**. No injectivity-preservation, no flatness.
82	- **Gap.** Route 2.ii is for `Sheaf J A` (fixed A, pointwise tensor). Crucially, Mathlib
83	  **does** ship the points for the relevant site: `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints (X : TopCat) :
84	  (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasEnoughPoints` (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Points`, Riou
85	  **2026** — "spaces have enough points, given by the stalks"). So the abstract `IsMonoidalW`
86	  instance gives `(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal` outright for *fixed-A pointwise* tensor on `X`'s
87	  site. What does NOT ship is a monoidal `SheafOfModules` for the *relative module* tensor
88	  `⊗_R`; the enough-points argument must be re-run by hand for the module localizer (the points
89	  themselves are free). **The technique is Mathlib-blessed AND its hardest input — enough
90	  points for `Opens X` — is already in Mathlib.**
91	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** for the module-level port — but the path is the
92	  enough-points/stalkwise-iso technique of Decision 3, NOT MonoidalClosed.
93	
94	### Decision 3: the minimal route — route (d), stalkwise-iso whiskering (needs NEITHER (a) NOR (b))
95	
96	The associator reduces (via the CLOSED bridge `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`, line 373) to
97	the two absorption isos being iso, i.e. to **`J.W (toPresheaf (η ▷ P.val))`** (and the mirror
98	`M.val ◁ η`), where `η = toSheafify ∈ J.W` (locally bijective) and `▷/◁` is the *module*
99	whiskering. Local **surjectivity** is free (right-exactness, already proven
100	`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`). The four ways to get the whole `J.W`:
101	
102	- **bundled-monoidal** (Decisions 1/2.i): `MonoidalClosed`. **DEAD** (multi-file absent).
103	- **(a) flat-exactness whiskering** (the realization `ts-assoc-gate210` recommended):
104	  splits `J.W` into locally-injective + locally-surjective and preserves injectivity via
105	  `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` — needs the **sectionwise** instance
106	  `[∀ U, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val(U))]`. **DEAD**: false for non-affine opens
107	  (⊗-invertibility is affine-local; the global-sections functor over a non-affine open is not
108	  exact). This is the iter-212 finding; the blueprint "Flatness is free" step
109	  (`.tex:664–669`) is the wrong step.
110	- **(b) `tensorObj_restrict_iso` local-trivialization**: needs the absent strong-monoidal
111	  presheaf pushforward (H1/H2, `tsroute208`/`mate207`). **DEAD / abandoned iter-209.**
112	- **(d) stalkwise-iso whiskering (RECOMMENDED — needs NEITHER (a) NOR (b)).**
113	  Do NOT split `J.W` into inj+surj. Use that **a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise isomorphism**
114	  (for the topological site of `X`, via `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`,
115	  `Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`, the concrete analogue of the abstract `hP.W_iff`). Then
116	  `(η ▷ P.val)_x = η_x ⊗_{𝒪_{X,x}} id_{P.val_x}`; since `η_x` is an **iso**, `η_x ⊗ id` is an
117	  iso (any functor preserves isos), so `η ▷ P.val` is stalkwise iso, hence in `J.W` —
118	  **for ARBITRARY `P`, no flatness, no invertibility, no local triviality, no restrict-iso.**
119	  The flat route needed flatness ONLY because it tried to preserve injectivity *alone*
120	  (`injective ⊗ id` needs flat); preserving the *combined* iso does not, because isos tensor
121	  to isos. This is exactly why Mathlib's enough-points `IsMonoidalW` needs no `MonoidalClosed`.
122	  - Porting ingredients (all flatness-free, points already in Mathlib):
123	    0. **Enough points for `X`'s site — PRESENT**: `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`
124	       (`Topology.Sheaves.Points`, 2026) + `hP.W_iff` (`Sites.Point.Conservative`) give
125	       `J.W f ↔ stalkwise iso`; concrete iso bridge `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`
126	       (`Topology.Sheaves.Stalks`; its instances `[PreservesFilteredColimits (forget C)]
127	       [HasLimits C] [PreservesLimits (forget C)] [(forget C).ReflectsIsomorphisms]` all hold
128	       for ModuleCat/AddCommGrp).
129	    1. **Run the stalkwise characterization for the MODULE-level `J.W`**: the abstract
130	       `IsMonoidalW` is fixed-A pointwise, so re-run the `hasEnoughPoints` + `hP.W_iff`
131	       argument for the relative `⊗_R` localizer; the `J.W ↔ IsIso(sheafify)` half is the
132	       file's own `inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`. Small project lemma.
133	    2. **Stalk commutes with the module-presheaf tensor**: `(M ⊗ᵖ N)_x ≅ M_x ⊗_{𝒪_{X,x}} N_x`.
134	       Stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight` preserve filtered colimits
135	       (the very instances the Mathlib `IsMonoidalW` block assumes, available for module
136	       categories). Project lemma, ~moderate, flatness-free.
137	- **Verdict**: route (d) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (technique)**: port the
138	  `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` stalkwise-iso argument concretely via `TopCat` stalks; reject (a),
139	  (b), bundled. Route (c) of the prior draft (local-on-cover injectivity scoped to
140	  `IsLocallyTrivial`) survives only as a narrower fallback if the stalk bridge (d.1) proves
141	  expensive.
142	
143	## Recommendation
144	
145	Q1/Q2: Mathlib ships **no** monoidal `SheafOfModules` and **no** monoidal sheafification for
146	the relative module tensor; the general `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` /
147	`presheafToSheaf.Monoidal` / `Localization.Monoidal` all reduce to `J.W.IsMonoidal`, which
148	Mathlib supplies **either** via `MonoidalClosed` (the wall, absent for modules) **or** via
149	`HasEnoughPoints` (`Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW`, 2026, **no MonoidalClosed**) — and the enough
150	points for `X`'s site **are** in Mathlib (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`, `Topology.Sheaves.Points`,
151	2026, via stalks). So this is genuine gap-fill, but the gap is *small and flatness-free* (re-run
152	the enough-points argument for the module localizer), not the multi-file `MonoidalClosed` build
153	prior iters assumed.
154	
155	Q3: the one route needing **NEITHER** (a) sectionwise flatness **NOR** (b)
156	`tensorObj_restrict_iso` is **route (d), stalkwise-iso whiskering**. Rewrite
157	`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` as `W_whisker_of_stalkwise` (drop the `[∀ X, Module.Flat …]`
158	hypothesis entirely): `J.W g → J.W (F ◁ g)` because a `J.W`-map is stalkwise iso and
159	`F_x ⊗ (iso)` is an iso. This closes `tensorObj_assoc_iso` for **arbitrary** `M N P` (it even
160	revives the blueprint's original "no flatness required" statement), and the existing 3-step
161	composite + `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` + `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` stay as-is.
162	
163	**Reversal signal: TRIGGERED in the favorable direction.** The prior verdict
164	(MonoidalClosed wall ⇒ flat route ⇒ now-dead) is overturned: the inj+surj decomposition was
165	the root-cause mistake, and a Mathlib-blessed flatness-free technique exists. First prover
166	objective: the stalkwise characterization of the module `J.W` on `Opens X` (d.1) +
167	stalk-tensor-commute (d.2), then the whisker rewrite. If d.1 turns out to be heavy
168	infrastructure, fall back to route (c) (scoped to `IsLocallyTrivial`, which the `OnProduct`
169	consumers already use).

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.RelPicFunctor
8	
9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.

29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.

94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
95	
96	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
97	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
98	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
99	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
100	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
101	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
102	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
103	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
104	
105	namespace PresheafOfModules
106	
107	universe v'
108	
109	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
113	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
114	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
115	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
116	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
117	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
118	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
119	  naturality {X Y} f := by
120	    ext r
121	    dsimp
122	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
123	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
124	    simp only [map_one]
125	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
129	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
130	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
131	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
132	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
133	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
134	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
135	  app X := by
136	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
137	  naturality {X Y} f := by
138	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
139	    dsimp
140	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
141	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
142	    rfl
143	
144	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
145	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
146	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
147	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
148	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
149	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
150	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
151	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
152	  μ_natural_left := by
153	    intro X Y f X'
154	    ext1 Z
155	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
156	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
157	  μ_natural_right := by
158	    intro X Y X' f
159	    ext1 Z
160	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
161	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
162	  associativity := by
163	    intro M N P
164	    ext1 Z
165	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
166	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
167	  left_unitality := by
168	    intro M
169	    ext1 Z
170	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
171	      (M.obj Z)
172	  right_unitality := by
173	    intro M
174	    ext1 Z
175	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
176	      (M.obj Z)

178	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
179	sheafification localizer
180	
181	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
182	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
183	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
184	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
185	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
186	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
187	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
188	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
189	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
190	structure. -/
191	
192	section FlatWhisker
193	
194	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
195	
196	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
197	
198	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
199	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
200	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
201	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
202	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
203	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
204	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
205	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
206	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
207	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
208	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
209	
210	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
211	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
212	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
213	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
214	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
215	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
216	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
217	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
218	
219	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
220	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
221	half. -/
222	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
223	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
224	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
225	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
226	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
227	  constructor
228	  intro U s
229	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
230	  | zero =>
231	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
232	      intro V i _
233	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
234	  | tmul a b =>
235	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
236	      intro V i hi
237	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
238	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
239	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
240	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
241	      congr 1
242	  | add s t hs ht =>
243	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
244	      intro V i hi
245	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
246	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
247	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
248	
249	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
250	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
251	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
252	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
253	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
254	injectivity half. -/
255	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
256	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
257	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
258	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
259	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
260	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
261	  constructor
262	  intro X ξ η h
263	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
264	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
265	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
266	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
267	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
268	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
269	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
270	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
271	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
272	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
273	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
274	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
275	  have hex : Function.Exact
276	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
277	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
278	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
279	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
280	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
281	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
282	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
283	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
284	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
285	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
286	    intro ζ
287	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
288	    | zero =>
289	        rw [map_zero]
290	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
291	    | tmul a kk =>
292	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
293	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
294	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
295	          rw [map_zero]
296	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
297	          exact kk.2
298	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
299	        intro V f hf
300	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
301	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
302	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
303	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
304	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
305	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
306	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
307	        rw [map_add]
308	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
309	        intro V f hf
310	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
311	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
312	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
313	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
314	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
315	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
316	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
317	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
318	  intro V f hf
319	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
320	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
321	  exact hf
322	
323	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
324	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
325	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
326	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
327	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
328	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
329	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
330	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
331	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
332	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
333	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
334	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
335	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
336	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
337	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
338	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
339	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
340	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
341	
342	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
343	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
344	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
345	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
346	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
347	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
348	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
349	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
350	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
351	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
352	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
353	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
354	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
355	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
356	  have hconj : g ▷ F
357	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
358	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
359	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
360	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
361	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
362	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
363	  exact hwl
364	
365	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
366	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
367	
368	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
369	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
370	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
371	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
372	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
373	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
374	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
375	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
376	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
377	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
378	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
379	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
380	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
381	
382	section WhiskerOfW
383	
384	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
385	
386	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
387	
388	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
389	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
390	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
391	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
392	
393	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
394	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
395	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
396	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
397	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
398	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
399	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
400	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
401	
402	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
403	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
404	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
405	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
406	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
407	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
408	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
409	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
410	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
411	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
412	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
413	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
414	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
415	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
416	  -- ROUTE (d) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
417	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); needs the module-level stalk/tensor port
418	  -- (d.1)+(d.2), Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. See docstring + task result.
419	  sorry
420	
421	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
422	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
423	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
424	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
425	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
426	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
427	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
428	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
429	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
430	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
431	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
432	  have hbij := hg
433	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
434	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
435	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
436	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
437	
438	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
439	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
440	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
441	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
442	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
443	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
444	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
445	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
446	  have hconj : g ▷ F
447	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
448	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
449	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
450	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
451	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
452	  exact hwl
453	
454	end WhiskerOfW

462	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
463	read at a single morphism. -/
464	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
465	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
466	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
467	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
468	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
469	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
470	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
471	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
472	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
473	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
474	  exact h2
